issues.title: 2.5.1 breaks /dev/dri detection - Hardware acceleration is dead
issues.next_action_reason: Long-standing GPU regression with promised-but-unshipped fix; confirm status and surface vgem workaround.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for the continued reports and the community diagnosis. Root cause: the vgem kernel module is no longer auto-loaded on the 6.6.x WSL2 kernel, so /dev/dri/cardN is missing and VA-API hardware acceleration fails. Workaround: create /etc/modules-load.d/vgem.conf containing 'vgem' (per zcobol/Quasar9000). @benhillis - you mentioned a fix was in progress in April; could you confirm whether auto-loading vgem is still planned so we can close the loop?

issues.title: WSL 2.7.3: /mnt/shared_memory missing → WSLg windows invisible ([WARN:COPY MODE])
issues.next_action_reason: Confirmed regression with a partial community workaround; acknowledge, and investigate why /mnt/shared_memory tmpfs isn't created on cold boot (COPY MODE fallback).
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks @neumaennl and @Perer876 — the fstab tmpfs workaround confirms the root cause: on WSL 2.7.3 `/mnt/shared_memory` isn't being created, so WSLg falls back to `[WARN:COPY MODE]` and nothing renders. The fact that it recurs after a Windows cold start (but a `wsl --shutdown` fixes it) points at a mount-ordering regression we need to fix in the mount setup rather than leave to fstab. Tracking this as a 2.7.3 regression.
